You can watch my full $NOW breakdown here including why I own it and my other favorite software name tied to this theme.

piped.video/MRGKv_tzyCA?si=vXxY…

$SPCX spending $60B on Cursor validates the idea that real AI value accrues to the application layer that owns the workflow, proprietary data loop and enterprise distribution.

$NOW is building exactly that as the orchestration layer for enterprise AI agents across business workflows.

The Cursor deal is a reminder that the market will pay massive premiums for software that owns where work actually happens.

Jim Cramer says $SPCX has become a “memestock” and warned investors not to chase it higher.

He said he’d hate to see the stock get walked toward $NVDA size through a series of overnight moves.

$MSFT is reportedly moving Copilot Cowork to usage-based pricing and preparing a lower-cost enterprise AI model hosted by Microsoft.

The cheaper DeepSeek-powered option could arrive in the coming weeks as Microsoft looks to make enterprise AI more flexible and cost efficient.

$GOOGL introduced Brazos which is a closed-loop liquid-to-air cooling system built to bring liquid cooling into existing air-cooled data centers.

As AI chips push beyond 1,000W, Brazos can support up to 60 kW per rack without forcing a full data center retrofit.

If I had to put my Elon hat on, $SPCX acquiring Cursor for $60B is about buying the top of the AI stack to protect the bottom.

If they only build data centers and sell raw compute then they're the landlord but if they own the application that consumes the compute then they also own the demand, the user relationship and the learning loop.

That learning loop is the real prize because every time developers use Cursor, they create signals around how code gets written and what enterprises actually need which can make xAI’s coding models better and pull more developers into the ecosystem over time.

I also think the $60B price tag validates the bigger point where the AI application layer can command massive premiums when it owns the user relationship, workflow and learning loop. 

That is also why I've been adding to $PLTR because SpaceX just paid $60B to acquire a compounding learning loop while Palantir already owns one through its ontology which gets stickier and more valuable with every customer interaction.

All portfolio performance and transaction data has been independently validated by a third party:

savvytrader.com/StockSavvySh…

$OSS up over 16% after securing an initial $8.4M defense AI server contract with platform value estimated ~$44M over four years.

This is OSS's third win with the same prime customer in eight months which is a sign its rugged AI servers are becoming embedded in the program.

While for AMD Helios, we continue to expect motherboard shipments in Q4'26, though full rack deployments are likely skewed toward YE'26 given additional time required for double-wide rack optimization, validation, and data center integration."

🚀 Art of the First Day: SpaceX Edition

$SPCX proved the power of the Art of the First Day.

It opened at $150, and never broke it. True sign of power for an IPO.

Put in a higher low, then broke out thru the $176.52 Day 1 high. That showed FOMO.

Then it cleared $188, and gapped up today and held it. Even more FOMO.

Hit $225.64 this morning. I'm down to 20% of my swing long.

This is why we follow technicals and not opinions. 

We all heard the valuation arguments but the price action sent another message from the start.

I'll be covering $SPCX in my free newsletter so make sure you follow along:

💌 t3live.com/rd

And once the stock is trading for more days and we have daily moving averages, we'll be doing more trend analysis work on it. 

In the meantime, let's focus on spotting FEAR and FOMO as they come.

You can watch an Art of the First Day video tutorial below.
$spy follow up as we have an inside day with specific stock action.

When mega-cap tech does the heavy lifting, investors confuse price strength with market strength.

That looks like calm returning.

But volatility getting cheaper after a scare is not the same thing as risk disappearing. It just means the market stopped paying up for insurance.
